bridge_concepts: Ferrihydrite surface-catalyzed lipid peroxidation rate constants

notes.ferroptosis_serpentinization: ~0 true co-occurrences (2 results are MeSH expansion false positives to asbestos/serpentine minerals); bridge is NOVEL
notes.ferrihydrite_lipid: PMID 31836519 directly studies ferrihydrite nanoparticles + lipid membrane interactions; partial precedent exists
notes.iron_mineral_lipid: 164 papers on iron-mineral-lipid oxidation in soil/environmental geochemistry; chemistry is established

kegg_connections: hsa04216 (ferroptosis) confirmed: GPX4, ACSL4, SLC7A11, TFRC, FTH1, NCOA4 are pathway members
GPX4 (hsa:2879) in pathways: hsa00480, hsa00590, hsa01100, hsa04216
ACSL4 (hsa:2180) in pathways: hsa04216, hsa00061, hsa00071, hsa01100, hsa01212, hsa03320, hsa04146, hsa04714, hsa04920
No KEGG pathway for geochemical/abiotic Fenton chemistry (expected — KEGG is biology-only)
Bridge operates at chemical reaction level, not KEGG pathway level

fenton_rate_ratio.verdict: PLAUSIBLE — same Fenton chemistry, rates differ by only 3x net

concentration_ratio.verdict: PLAUSIBLE — higher concentration SUPPORTS bridge (geochemical system exceeds ferroptosis threshold)

propagation_kinetics.verdict: PLAUSIBLE — propagation kinetics strongly enhanced at serpentinization T

timescale_notes.warning: Generator must specify lab-scale experiments, not geological timescales

critical_asymmetry.warning: GPX4/GSH enzymatic protection is NOT present in abiotic systems. Generator must frame bridge as shared Fenton/PLOOH CHEMISTRY, not shared enzymatic regulation.

string_interactions: GPX4 top partners: GSR (0.980), PRDX6 (0.973), HSPA5 (0.952), GSTO2 (0.946) — all antioxidant network
ACSL4-GPX4 interaction score: 0.834 (high confidence) — confirms ferroptosis ACSL4→PLOOH→GPX4 axis
ACSL4 top partner FASN: 0.969 — fatty acid synthesis link
No high-confidence interactions between ferroptosis proteins and mineral-binding proteins (expected — STRING is biology-only)
plausibility_rationale: Chemistry bridge (Fenton/PLOOH) is sound and supported by calculations. Timescales are compatible at lab scale. Iron concentrations favor geochemical peroxidation. Critical warning: GPX4/GSH enzymatic axis is not transferable — generator must frame bridge as universal abiotic chemistry that biology evolved to suppress, not as parallel enzymatic regulation.
generator_warnings: DO NOT claim GPX4 equivalents in serpentinization systems
DO NOT compare to geological timescales without temperature normalization
DO frame as: Fenton/PLOOH chemistry is universal; ferroptosis evolved GPX4/GSH to suppress it
DO highlight ferrihydrite as the geochemical mineral catalyst (PMID 31836519 precedent)
DO use laboratory serpentinization experiments (150-300 C, 24-96h) as the experimental bridge
1333x higher Fe(II) in serpentinization fluids SUPPORTS peroxidation — emphasize this
